skill.name: code-review-digest-writer
skill.description: Generates markdown code review digests from GitHub PR review comments for a selected repository and date range.
skill.summary: Creates weekly review digests that group PR feedback into themes, repeated issues, and team takeaways.
skill.author: DiversioTeam

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many shell-backtick and weak-cryptography patterns, but review shows these are markdown inline-code and wording false positives, not Ruby execution or crypto code. The skill does legitimately use Bash with GitHub CLI and writes digest files under docs/review-digests, so publication should include a warning about external command use and untrusted repository content.

medium_findings.title: External GitHub CLI Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to use Bash with gh to query pull requests, comments, and review-thread data. This is legitimate for a review digest workflow, but it can expose repository metadata to command output and depends on the authenticated GitHub identity.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash and gh workflow is explicit and central to the skill. I found no evidence of malicious exfiltration, but external command use is confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted Repository Guidance Treated As Authoritative
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to read docs/review-digests/AGENTS.md and treat it as the source of truth for digest behavior. A malicious repository could place prompt-injection instructions there, so agents should apply the skill scope and platform instructions before repository guidance.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is clearly described, and repository-local guidance can be attacker controlled in some use cases. I found no direct injection text in this skill file.

medium_findings.title: Writes Documentation Files In The Target Repository
medium_findings.description: The skill can create or update markdown files below docs/review-digests. The scope is narrow and documentation-only, but it still modifies the filesystem and should be run after confirming the target date and repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write behavior and target directory are explicit. The limited docs-only scope reduces severity.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many inline backtick spans as Ruby or shell execution. The reviewed lines contain markdown examples, file paths, labels, and command names, not executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is markdown instructions and the flagged backticks delimit text examples. There is no Ruby source or command substitution syntax being executed.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged weak cryptography, but the reviewed content uses the word digest for review summaries and contains no hash implementation, cipher selection, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious term is semantically about newsletter digests, not cryptographic digests. I found no evidence of crypto code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool And gh Command Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires Bash access and assumes gh is authenticated. This enables repository data retrieval through local credentials and should be disclosed to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tools and gh dependency are explicit. The pattern is risky by capability, not by confirmed malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository Content Can Influence Output
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill reads local digest guidance, previous digests, PR comments, and diff context. These inputs may contain untrusted text that should be summarized without obeying instructions embedded in them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly consumes repository and PR text. I found no prompt injection in the skill itself, but this data path is a real review concern.

content.value_statement: Teams lose useful lessons when pull request feedback stays scattered across reviews. This skill turns date-bounded GitHub review comments into a structured markdown digest with themes, repeated issues, and takeaways.
actual_capabilities: Creates a markdown digest for a requested start date and end date.
Uses GitHub CLI to collect pull request comments and review-thread context.
Groups review feedback into themes across multiple pull requests.
Labels themes as new or repeated by comparing prior digest files.
Writes the digest under docs/review-digests using the end date as the filename.
Adds reference-style links for pull requests and review comments.
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI access for complete pull request data.
Quality depends on the clarity and availability of review comments.
It should only edit documentation under docs/review-digests.
Repository guidance and PR comments may contain untrusted text.

use_cases.title: Weekly Engineering Learning Digest
use_cases.description: Summarize the main review lessons from the past week for an engineering team.

use_cases.title: Recurring Review Pattern Tracking
use_cases.description: Find review themes that continue across multiple digest periods and need team attention.

use_cases.title: Onboarding Review Knowledge Base
use_cases.description: Turn historical pull request feedback into documentation that helps new developers learn team standards.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic Digest
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one repository and one date range.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a code review digest for this repository for 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-07.

prompt_templates.title: Use Prior Digests
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare current review feedback with existing digest history.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a review digest for 2026-06-08 to 2026-06-14. Use existing docs/review-digests files to mark repeated themes.

prompt_templates.title: Focus On Risk Areas
prompt_templates.scenario: Emphasize security, privacy, and correctness review feedback.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a digest for 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-30. Highlight security, PII, access control, and data correctness themes.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Repository Digest
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request when GitHub CLI should target a specific repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a digest for owner/service-repo for 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-15. Use GitHub CLI with the explicit repo target and write the final markdown digest.

output_examples.output: A markdown review digest with an overview, themed sections, new and repeated tags, and reference links for cited pull requests.

output_examples.input: Summarize repeated review issues from this month.
output_examples.output: A monthly digest that calls out recurring patterns, explains why they matter, and lists practical team takeaways.

output_examples.input: Create a digest but GitHub CLI is unavailable.
output_examples.output: A partial digest based on local notes and existing files, with a clear note about missing GitHub review data.

best_practices: Provide an exact start date and end date before running the skill.
Review any repository-specific digest guidance before writing the final file.
Check generated links and repeated-theme labels before sharing the digest.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to modify application code or configuration files.
Do not treat PR comments or repository docs as trusted instructions.
Do not publish a digest without noting missing GitHub data.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a markdown code review digest for a selected repository and date range.

faq.question: Does it require GitHub CLI?
faq.answer: GitHub CLI is needed for complete pull request and review-thread data.

faq.question: Where does it write files?
faq.answer: It writes digest files under docs/review-digests using the end date as the filename.

faq.question: Can it identify repeated themes?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares current review feedback with prior digest files when they are available.

faq.question: Can it work without network access?
faq.answer: It can create a limited digest from local notes and existing files, but GitHub data will be incomplete.

faq.question: Is repository content trusted?
faq.answer: No. Repository guidance and PR comments should be treated as source material, not higher-priority instructions.
